summary: Large Venom stage decompilation PR focused on src/melee/gr/grvenom.c, adding many previously stubbed or asm-marked functions and improving several near-matches. The progress report showed 12 new full matches in main/melee/gr/grvenom, including grVenom_802040F0, grVenom_80204B88, grVenom_80204DD4, grVenom_80203EAC, grVenom_80204CEC, grVenom_80206BF0, grVenom_80206D7C, grVenom_80205E84, grVenom_802056B0, grVenom_80206D10, grVenom_80206CB0, and fn_802040B4, plus 10 partial improvements such as grVenom_8020362C at 81.23% and several 92–99% near-matches. The PR also tightened grCorneria helper prototypes needed by Venom code. Review feedback flagged quality concerns: some matches used too many labels, suspicious raw offset/GObj casts, avoidable extern declarations, missing proper headers, and uncertain argument/struct types.

key_files.role: Primary Venom stage implementation; added most decompiled functions, local temporary structs, extern data symbols, includes, callbacks, lighting, spawn, stage object, and helper logic.
Venom header updated, though exact header diff details are weak in the supplied excerpt.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes for grCorneria_801E2598, grCorneria_801E25C4, and grCorneria_801E2738 so Venom code could call shared Corneria-style helpers.

what_changed: Added many includes to grvenom.c, including grdisplay, lb_00B0, lb_00F9, lbaudio_ax, mplib, baselib gobjgxlink, baselib gobjproc, and baselib random.

Introduced local temporary Venom data structs such as grVe_Data, grVe_ExtData, grVe_TimingData, grVe_Lighting, grVe_GroundData, VenomSpawnData, and GroundVenom_ForLighting, with comments noting several should move to gr/types.h, grvenom.h, or another proper header.
Declared Venom data symbols including grVe_803E5348, grVe_804D6A30, grVe_804D6A34, grVe_804D6A38, grVe_804D6A40, several grVe_804DB7xx/804DB73x constants, and color constants used by lighting code.

Implemented stage object creation helper grVenom_80203EAC using Ground_801C14D0, StageCallbacks from grVe_803E5348 offset 0x44, GObj_SetupGXLink, callback assignment, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and OSReport fallback.

Implemented Venom stage initialization grVenom_80203B18 as a partial match, including object creation, stage_info flags, random timing setup through HSD_Randi, mpLib_80057BC0 calls, lighting object animation loop flag setup, and calls to Ground_801C39C0/Ground_801C3BB4.

Implemented fn_802040B4 and grVenom_802040F0 for a Venom ground object setup path, including animation calls, callback registration with Ground_801C10B8, storing JObj pointers in gv.venom fields, comparing JObj world positions with lb_8000B1CC, and setting ground flags.

Implemented or partially implemented object update/setup functions such as grVenom_80203FD4, grVenom_80204284, grVenom_80204428, grVenom_80204CEC, grVenom_80204DD4, grVenom_802052E0, grVenom_802053B0, grVenom_80206BF0, grVenom_80206CB0, grVenom_80206D10, and grVenom_80206D7C.

Added Venom lighting update logic in grVenom_80204B88 using GXColor constants and Ground_801C05xx color setters; comments indicate the existing grVenom_GroundVars xE0 type was probably wrong because the function treats it as bitfields rather than f32.

Changed grcorneria.h helper declarations from unknown signatures to typed prototypes: grCorneria_801E2598(u32, u32) returning s32, grCorneria_801E25C4(u32*, int, int, int), and grCorneria_801E2738(HSD_GObj*, void*, u32, u32).
Author noted the PR was WIP and that the permuter was being run on 99% matches before review.
smart_moves: Used shared stage/Cornelia helper prototypes instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, enabling typed calls from Venom code and reducing ambiguity at callsites.

Marked uncertain local structs with TODO comments rather than presenting them as finalized layout knowledge; this preserves matching progress while warning future workers that header/type work remains.
Captured match quality directly in comments for near-matches such as 81.23%, 88.51%, 92.15%, 97.29%, 99.70%, 99.90%, and 99.92%, making the remaining matching problems searchable.

Used existing stage/ground infrastructure patterns where visible: Ground_801C14D0 for object creation, StageCallbacks callback slots, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, GET_GROUND/GET_JOBJ macros, grAnime calls, and mpLib calls.
Expanded JObj scale setter behavior manually in grVenom_80204DD4 to reproduce assert lines and dirty-matrix side effects, which is a useful matching tactic when inlines do not produce exact output.
The author explicitly planned to run the permuter on 99% matches before requesting final review, which is appropriate for late-stage register allocation/control-flow cleanup.
decomp_lessons: Do not treat a high percentage match as sufficient if the C is structurally poor; reviewer feedback called out excessive jump labels and suspicious raw offset use as a sign the match may not be a good decompilation.
Temporary local struct overlays can unblock progress, but they should be moved into proper shared headers once enough layout is known, especially for stage data like grVe_Data and grVenom_GroundVars.

When a function uses assert line numbers from jobj.h, include the correct JObj header and use the established inline/macro when possible; converting the assert line such as x294 to decimal can identify the intended inline.
Prefer including the correct header over adding standalone extern function declarations; reviewers explicitly objected to unnecessary extern declarations.

For repeated stage helper functions, compare against equivalent functions in every stage. Reviewer noted one function pattern exists in every stage with slight modifications and suspected the first parameter should be Vec3*.
Ground variable unions can hide wrong type assumptions: this PR used casts and even mutecity2 bitfields to access Venom storage, and comments/review indicate grVenom_GroundVars needs correction.

Be cautious with raw field offsets such as +0x2C because they may actually be typed objects like GObj pointers; replacing raw offset arithmetic with real struct fields improves both correctness and reviewability.

Lighting/environment state functions may reveal bitfields in existing structs previously typed as f32; use call behavior and flag operations to revise struct definitions rather than adding permanent workaround structs.
naming_conventions: Venom stage functions use the grVenom_8020XXXX address-based naming convention until semantic names are known.
Static/local generated helper retained fn_802040B4 naming, matching address-style decomp convention for unnamed local functions.
Venom data symbols follow grVe_80XXXXXX naming, e.g. grVe_803E5348, grVe_804D6A30, and grVe_804DB740.
Temporary structs were named with grVe_ or Venom prefixes, such as grVe_Data, grVe_ExtData, grVe_TimingData, grVe_Lighting, grVe_GroundData, VenomSpawnData, and GroundVenom_ForLighting.
Header prototypes for related Corneria helpers kept the grCorneria_801EXXXX names but gained typed signatures instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used permuter work on 99% matches according to the author comment.
Manual expansion of HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y in grVenom_80204DD4 reproduced assert checks, direct scale writes, flag tests, HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty, and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub calls.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in some functions such as grVenom_80206BF0 and grVenom_80204CEC to preserve stack layout.
Used temporary pointer aliases and casts into grVe_803E5348 to match data-table indexing and register behavior in functions such as grVenom_802052E0 and grVenom_802053B0.
Used explicit bitfield overlay structs for xE0 lighting state to match flag manipulation when the canonical grVenom_GroundVars type was suspected wrong.
Introduced labels/goto in grVenom_80203B18 for control-flow matching, but this was criticized by review as not a good-looking match when overused.
Matched object callback setup by indexing StageCallbacks from grVe_803E5348 at offset 0x44 and invoking callback0/callback2/callback3 paths.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested deleting the comment line '// Decompilation of fn_802040B4', implying such redundant decompilation comments are not desired.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Criticized one large function as not good-looking because it introduced many jump labels and used raw '+ 0x2C' accesses likely representing a GObj; concluded it was not a good match.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: For JObj inline/assert code, advised referencing jobj.h and converting x294 to decimal to identify the correct function to use for inlining.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Said an extern declaration should not be needed and the correct header should be included instead.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that a function pattern appears in every stage with slight modifications and believed the first parameter is a Vec3*.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move grVe_Data, grVe_ExtData, grVe_TimingData, VenomSpawnData, or GroundVenom_ForLighting into gr/types.h or grvenom.h.
Search for later corrections to grVenom_GroundVars, especially xE0, xC4 through xDC fields, and whether xE0 became a bitfield instead of f32.
Look for follow-up cleanup of grVenom_8020362C, grVenom_80203B18, grVenom_802053B0, and other partial matches called out as 81–99% in this PR.
Search for removal of unnecessary extern declarations in grvenom.c and replacement with proper includes such as jobj.h or relevant mpLib/ground headers.
Compare grVenom_80206D7C against equivalent functions in other stage files to confirm the suspected Vec3* first parameter.
Check whether the reviewer concern about excessive jump labels and raw +0x2C/GObj access was addressed before or after merge.
